elements.sent-2411-001: We continue the program initiated in \cite{inglis2011u, qiu2024some and study $L^1$-type functional inequalities for some probability measures defined in terms of homogeneous norms on a stratified Lie group, our goal being to obtain isoperimetric inequalities beyond the nondegenerate setting of probability measures defined in terms of the Carnot-Carath\'eodory distance.}
elements.sent-2411-002: We then provide some examples on and beyond stratified Lie groups.
elements.sent-2411-003: Primary 26D10, 60J60 Super-Poincar\'e inequality, Hardy inequality, $F$-Sobolev inequality, isoperimetric inequality, stratified Lie group.
elements.sent-2411-004: In this paper, we continue the program initiated in \cite{inglis2011u, qiu2024some and study $L^1\Phi$-entropy type inequalities for probability measures defined on stratified Lie groups using an adaptation of the methods presented in the latter, our goal being to obtain isoperimetric inequalities for some probability measures defined in terms of homogeneous norms.}

elements.sent-2411-006: It was shown in \cite[Corollary~4.1]{hebisch2010coercive that if $\psi$ is the Carnot-Carath\'eodory distance $d$ on the Heisenberg group $\bbG = \bbH^n$ and $p \geq 2$ then $\mu_p$ satisfies a $q$-logarithmic Sobolev inequality \begin{equation}\label{qlsi} \int \abs{f}^q\log\left(\frac{\abs{f}^q}{\int \abs{f}^qd\mu_p}\right)d\mu_p \lesssim \int \abs{\nabla_\bbG f}^qd\mu_p\end{equation} for $q \in (1, 2]$ H\"older conjugate to $p$ and $f: \bbH^n \rightarrow \bbR$ smooth.}

elements.sent-2411-170: We prove $q$-super-Poincar\'e inequalities, $q \in [1, 2]$, for a class of exponential power type probability measures defined in terms of a norm in a number of subelliptic settings, primarily on stratified Lie groups but also in the Grushin and Heisenberg-Greiner settings.

elements.sent-2411-174: The most striking feature of our results is that while our motivating examples of measures have supergaussian tails, the isoperimetric inequality they satisfy, which is generically optimal up to constants, corresponds to that of the subgaussian exponential power type measure $d\nu_r = Z^{-1 e^{-\abs{x ^r dx$, $r \in (1, 2)$ regarded as a measure on the metric space $(\bbR^n, \abs{\cdot )$.}}}}
